titleStmt.title: SII 40.250: original edition by K. Munirathnam
No. 250. (A.R. No. B. 295 of 1933.) Mādala, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On a pillar set up in the compound of the Sakalēśvarasvāmi temple.
This is dated 1073, Māgha, ba. 15, Thursday, Solar eclipse, corresponding to February 7, 1152 A.D. However the cyclic year was Prajāpati.

Records the gift of 55 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp to the god Sakalēśvaradēva by Velamuna Rēkama, who was the grand daughter of Pōti Nāyaka of Siri-Buddhāmu, and the favorite servant of the king. The gift was entrusted to Vīrabōya, son of Māḍibōya for supply of ghee to the temple.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded in TEI/EpiDoc according to the DHARMA Encoding Guide for Documentary Editions, version 2

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śakavarṣaṁbulu 1073 gunēṁṭi mā
kha bahala paṁccādaśiyu guruvāramuna sū
ryyagrahaṇa nimityamuna sirebuddāmuna pōtinā
yaku manmaṟālu śrīmanmahāmaṁḍalēśvara śrī vēlanā
ṁṭi kulōttuṁgga cōḍagoṁkkarājula Iṣṭavrittyaṟālu
vēlamunarēkama tamatallitaṁḍrulaku dharmmagā sakalē
śvaradēvarakakhaṁḍavartti dīpaṁbunakiccina goṟiyalu
55 vīniṁjēkoni Iṁddulaku māḍibōyani koḍku
vīrabōyuṁḍu dana putrānupautrikamācaṁdrarkka
mugā māneṁḍu nēyi vōyaṁgalavāru
Digital edition of SII 40.250 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
